title: Gavi supported countries
subtitle: Gavi provides financial and expert knowledge support for countries to access new and underused vaccines. Support is given to countries with a Gross National Income per capita of less than US$1,810, calculated using the World Bank Atlas Method, over the past three years.
caption:
Gavi supported countries, 2024

Gavi provides financial and expert knowledge support for countries to access new and underused vaccines. Support is given to countries with a Gross National Income per capita of less than US$1,810, calculated using the World Bank Atlas Method, over the past three years.

This choropleth map displays the global distribution of countries eligible for Gavi support in 2024, distinguishing nations based on a Gross National Income per capita threshold of less than US$1,810. The visualization uses a binary color scheme where teal indicates eligibility for support and light beige denotes ineligibility. The geographic pattern shows a high concentration of eligible nations across the African continent, covering the majority of sub-Saharan Africa including Nigeria, the Democratic Republic of the Congo, Ethiopia, and Kenya. In Asia, eligibility extends across a band of countries including Pakistan, Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Myanmar, and Yemen, along with nations in Central Asia such as Tajikistan and Kyrgyzstan. In the Pacific region, Papua New Guinea and several smaller island nations appear in the eligible category. Conversely, the map shows that North America, South America, Europe, Australia, and most of East Asia fall into the not eligible category, appearing in light beige. Large economies such as the United States, Brazil, China, and India are not highlighted as eligible under this specific income metric. The figures exclude international aviation and shipping from the income calculations. The visual distribution places the highest density of supported countries in low-income regions of Africa and South Asia, while higher-income regions remain uncolored.
